ucs.title: [LLM] Cloud Atlas (Inception) RTF remote-template injection chain via Office → EQNEDT32/mshta
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Cloud Atlas/Inception delivery chain: a spearphishing RTF/DOCX that fetches a remote template (T1221) over HTTP/WebDAV and then triggers either the CVE-2017-11882 Equation Editor exploit or an HTA dropper. The combination of Office reaching out for a template followed by EQNEDT32.EXE or mshta.exe child execution within seconds is highly characteristic of this actor and rare in benign traffic.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("eqnedt32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","regsvr32.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process_name Processes.process_id Processes.process Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename parent_process_id as office_pid host as src | join type=inner src office_pid [| tstats summariesonly=t count as net_count values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as remote_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as remote_port values(All_Traffic.app) as app from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe") AND All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,445) AND NOT (All_Traffic.dest_category="microsoft" OR All_Traffic.dest="*.office.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*.microsoft.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*.officeapps.live.com") by host All_Traffic.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename process_id as office_pid host as src] | where net_count > 0 | eval risk_note="Office fetched remote template/payload then spawned LOLBin within window" | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime src user parent_process_name process_name process remote_ip remote_port risk_note
ucs.kql:
let officeProcs = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe"]);
let lolbins   = dynamic(["eqnedt32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let msDomains = dynamic(["microsoft.com","office.com","office.net","officeapps.live.com","sharepoint.com","live.com","windows.com","office365.com"]);
let officeNet =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeProcs)
    | where RemotePort in (80,443,445)
    | where not(RemoteUrl has_any (msDomains))
    | where isnotempty(RemoteIP)
    | project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, OfficePid=InitiatingProcessId, OfficeName=InitiatingProcessFileName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeProcs)
| where FileName in~ (lolbins)
| project SpawnTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, OfficePid=InitiatingProcessId, OfficeName=InitiatingProcessFileName, ChildName=FileName, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ChildSha256=SHA256
| join kind=inner officeNet on DeviceId, OfficePid
| where datetime_diff('second', SpawnTime, NetTime) between (-30 .. 600)
| extend Note = strcat("Cloud Atlas chain: ", OfficeName, " -> ", RemoteUrl, " -> ", ChildName)
| project SpawnTime, DeviceName, AccountName, OfficeName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, ChildName, ChildCmd, Note
ucs.rationale: Inception/Cloud Atlas has used remote-template injection plus the Equation Editor exploit (CVE-2017-11882) and HTA droppers as their dominant delivery pattern across multiple campaigns from 2018 through 2022 (Kaspersky/Symantec/Unit42 reporting). Legitimate Office workflows do not retrieve templates over SMB/WebDAV from non-Microsoft hosts and then immediately spawn EQNEDT32 or mshta — pairing the

ucs.title: [LLM] Cloud Atlas VBShower/PowerShower polymorphic loader: mshta + WebDAV cloud-storage C2 + Run-key persistence
ucs.description: Detects the post-exploitation behavior of Cloud Atlas's VBShower / PowerShower implants: mshta.exe (or wscript) launched from Office, persistence written to HKCU\...\Run pointing at a VBS/HTA dropper, and beacons to consumer cloud-storage / WebDAV endpoints (CloudMe, OpenDrive, 4shared, pCloud) used as the actor's signature C2 channel — the very behavior the 'Cloud Atlas' name was coined for.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe") AND (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe") OR match(Processes.process,"(?i)\.(hta|vbs|vbe|js)")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as src | join type=inner src [| tstats summariesonly=t count as reg_hits values(Registry.registry_key_name) as reg_key values(Registry.registry_value_data) as reg_value from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path IN ("*\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\*","*\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce\\*") AND match(Registry.registry_value_data,"(?i)(\.vbs|\.vbe|\.hta|mshta|wscript|cscript|powershell)") by host | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | rename host as src] | join type=inner src [| tstats summariesonly=t count as c2_count values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2_host from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","svchost.exe") AND (All_Traffic.dest="*cloudme.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*opendrive.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*4shared.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*pcloud.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*yandex.*" OR All_Traffic.dest="webdav.*") by host | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename host as src] | eval rationale="VBShower/PowerShower triad: HTA/VBS exec + Run-key + WebDAV cloud-storage C2" | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime src user process_name process reg_key reg_value c2_host rationale
ucs.kql:
let scripty = dynamic(["mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe"]);
let cloudC2 = dynamic(["cloudme.com","opendrive.com","4shared.com","pcloud.com","yandex.","webdav.","box.com","mail.ru"]);
let execs =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where FileName in~ (scripty)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe")
          or ProcessCommandLine has_any (".hta",".vbs",".vbe","vbscript:","javascript:")
    | project ExecTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ChildName=FileName, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ParentName=InitiatingProcessFileName;
let persist =
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where ActionType == "RegistryValueSet"
    | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\CurrentVersion\RunOnce")
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (".vbs",".vbe",".hta","mshta","wscript","cscript","powershell")
    | project RegTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData;
let beacon =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","svchost.exe")
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (cloudC2) or RemoteUrl has "webdav"
    | project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, NetProc=InitiatingProcessFileName;
execs
| join kind=inner persist on DeviceId
| join kind=inner beacon on DeviceId
| where datetime_diff('hour', NetTime, ExecTime) between (-72 .. 72)
| extend Verdict = "Cloud Atlas VBShower/PowerShower triad: script exec + Run-key persistence + cloud-storage C2"
| project ExecTime, DeviceName, AccountName, ParentName, ChildName, ChildCmd, RegistryKey, RegistryValueData, RemoteUrl, NetProc, Verdict
ucs.rationale: Cloud Atlas earned its Kaspersky-cluster name from its consistent abuse of consumer cloud-storage / WebDAV endpoints (CloudMe was the canonical example) for staging and C2, paired with VBShower (HTA/VBS) and PowerShower (PowerShell) implants persisted via HKCU Run keys. Any single leg is noisy, but the three-way join — script interpreter spawned from Office, Run-key persistence pointing at script
